name: Henry Louis Gates, Jr.
description: American literary critic, professor and historian (born 1950)
abstract: Henry Louis Gates Jr. (born September 16, 1950), popularly known by his childhood nickname "Skip", is an American literary critic, professor, historian, and filmmaker who serves as the Alphonse Fletcher University Professor and the director of the Hutchins Center for African and African American Research at Harvard University.

about_quotes.speaker: Greg Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: the genetic works that Henry Louis Gates is doing through some companies, and these shows that he has on PBS where he looks at the genetic history of usually famous people shows that these mixtures are just reality.
about_quotes.doc_title: Greg Thomas interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 11, 2014
